title: DataQuality
content:
# DataQuality


## Properties

- **resource** (`string`): The Asset Data Stream pair to which the DataQuality is applied. (example: `krn:ad:asset1/setpoint`).
- **configurations** (`object`): The Data Quality configurations applied to the Asset Data Stream pair.
- **created** (`string`): UTC time when the data quality was created, formatted in RFC 3339.
- **created_by** (`string`): The process that created this data quality. This can be a user or an automated process like a workload, application, etc.
- **updated** (`string`): UTC time when any data quality keys were last updated, formatted in RFC 3339.
- **updated_by** (`string`): The process that last updated this data quality. This can be a user or an automated process like a workload, application, etc.
summary: Object with properties: resource, configurations, created, created_by, updated

resource.description: The Asset Data Stream pair to which the DataQuality is applied. (example: `krn:ad:asset1/setpoint`).

configurations.description: The Data Quality configurations applied to the Asset Data Stream pair.

enabled.description: Define if the timestamp anomaly algorithm is enabled.
Define if the stale detection algorithm is enabled.

configuration.description: The configuration for the stale detection algorithm.

window_interval.description: The size of the window to check for stale data.

window_interval_unit.description: The unit of time for the window interval. This is used in data availability and stale detection checks to define the time period over which data is expected.

enabled.description: Define if the out of range detection algorithm is enabled.

configuration.description: The configuration for the out of range detection algorithm.

min_threshold.description: The minimum threshold for the out of range detection.

max_threshold.description: The maximum threshold for the out of range detection.

enabled.description: Define if the outlier detection algorithm is enabled.

configuration.description: The configuration for the outlier detection algorithm.

model.description: The model to use for outlier detection. This is used in the outlier detection algorithm to determine how to identify outliers in the data.

z_score.description: The configuration for the z-score outlier detection model.

window_size.description: The size of the window to check for outliers.

enabled.description: Define if the data availability algorithm is enabled.

configuration.description: The configuration for the data availability algorithm.

window_interval_unit.description: The unit of time for the window interval. This is used in data availability and stale detection checks to define the time period over which data is expected.

created.description: UTC time when the data quality was created, formatted in RFC 3339.

created_by.description: The process that created this data quality. This can be a user or an automated process like a workload, application, etc.

updated.description: UTC time when any data quality keys were last updated, formatted in RFC 3339.

updated_by.description: The process that last updated this data quality. This can be a user or an automated process like a workload, application, etc.
